verses.text: The LORD: command Israel to put out of the camp every leper, every one with an issue, and whoever is defiled by the dead — male and female. Put them out, that they defile not the camp where I dwell. Israel did so. Guidance: God's presence in the camp raises the bar. The unclean are kept outside — not because they are despised, but because His presence is dangerous holiness. They are still loved, still come back when cleansed.

When a man or woman sin against the LORD, that soul is guilty. Confess; restore the principal and add a fifth to the wronged party. If no kinsman to receive, give to the priest with a ram of atonement. All heave offerings belong to the priest. Guidance: Restitution comes before sacrifice. Make the neighbor whole first — full payment plus twenty percent. Only then can the altar receive what you bring. Forgiveness walks through fairness.

If a man's wife go aside, the act hidden, and the spirit of jealousy comes on her husband — he brings her to the priest with an offering of barley meal, no oil, no frankincense; an offering of jealousy bringing iniquity to remembrance. Guidance: A husband who suspects but cannot prove brings his wife — and his suspicion — to God. The ordeal protects the wife from being killed on a jealous hunch. Suspicion is taken out of his hands and laid in God's.

Priest brings her before the LORD. Takes holy water in an earthen vessel, dust from the tabernacle floor, puts it in. Uncovers her head, puts the memorial offering in her hands. Priest holds the bitter water. Adjures her: if no man has lain with thee, be free; if gone aside, the LORD make thy thigh rot. She: Amen. Guidance: She speaks the Amen herself. God, not the husband, decides. If she has been faithful, the water won't harm her. The court she enters is higher than his.

Priest writes the curses and blots them out with the bitter water; causes her to drink. Takes the offering, waves it, burns the memorial on the altar. If defiled, the water turns bitter and her body shows it; if clean, she is free, and shall conceive seed. Guidance: The curse is washed off the page and into her cup. Either God's judgment will act, or she will go home cleared — and possibly more fertile. Either outcome silences the jealousy forever.

This is the law of jealousies, when a wife goes aside, or when the spirit of jealousy comes upon a husband. The priest shall execute upon her all this law. Then the man shall be guiltless, and the woman bears her own iniquity. Guidance: The marriage is brought before God's bench rather than handled at home. Sometimes the only way to end a jealousy storm is to take both parties out of the storm and put them in the sanctuary.
